title: Leaning Temple of Huma
tagline: Leaning Temple of Huma — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: The Leaning Temple of Huma in India is one of only a very few leaning temples in the world.
overview.description: The Leaning Temple of Huma in India is one of only a very few leaning temples in the world. It is located in Huma, a village situated on the bank of the Mahanadi, 23 km (14 mi) south of Sambalpur in the Indian state of Odisha. The temple is dedicated to the Hindu god Lord Bimaleshwar. It is unknown if this structure is leaning by design or for another reason.

topPlaces.name: Kadobahal
topPlaces.description: Kadobahal is a village in Attabira tehsil, Bargarh District, Odisha, India. This village is situated on a side of State Highway No.

topPlaces.name: Chipilima Hydro Electric Project
topPlaces.description: Chipilima Hydro Electric Project (CHEP) is a hydropower plant located at Chipilima, Sambalpur, Odisha, India. The Odisha government has plans for the modernisation of 5th and 6th unit of Burla Hydro Electric Project (BHEP) and 3rd unit of Chipilima Hydro Electric Project (CHEP) during the 11th five year plan tenure.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Leaning Temple of Huma?
faq.a: The best time to visit Leaning Temple of Huma is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Leaning Temple of Huma?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Swami Vivekananda Airport, Raipur (226 km) and the nearest railway station is Sambalpur Junction (22 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Leaning Temple of Huma?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Leaning Temple of Huma at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Leaning Temple of Huma?
faq.a: Stays in Leaning Temple of Huma start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Leaning Temple of Huma?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kadobahal, Chipilima Hydro Electric Project.
